steps.title: Make two exponential data rows
steps.content: This is a good Desmos exponential-regression shortcut. The value is $200$ when $x=0$. Each time $x$ increases by $1$, $y$ is multiplied by $3$, so the next value is $200\cdot3=600$ when $x=1$.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: Use the Desmos row $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. It returns $a=200$ and $b=3$, so the model is $y=200(3)^x$.

steps.title: Match the choices
steps.content: Choice A and choice C are power functions because $x$ is the base, so they do not multiply by the same factor each time $x$ increases by $1$. Choice D is exponential, but its initial value is $3$ and its multiplier is $200$. Choice B, $y=200(3)^x$, matches both regression parameters. Choice B is correct.
